All right, welcome back. In this lesson, we're going to go over three primary tools for coaching the conversion, in particular, in the world of automated intimacy. So let's begin. The three tools we're going to be looking at today are called touch point theory, which is another way of saying gradually dialing up the intimacy and dynamism of our marketing and our exchanges as we get closer to that finish line, closer to point buyer, closer to our earned reality and essentially escalating our investment of time, energy and attention as our prospect escalates their investment in us, our program and them converting into a buyer. Next, it's going to be earned enrollment, which is a concept where prospects graduate themselves from one automated conversion ecosystem to another or one phase of a funnel to another through well earning it. So we don't ascend them as much as they ascend themselves and we'll talk more about why we do that. But the bottom line is it's more of a balanced exchange and people value what they earn versus what is just given to them or worse pushed upon them. Next we're going to get into the idea of conversion contexts via automated intimacy. So this is a new tool we're introducing to the coaching the conversion ethos, if you will, that has only really been made possible through automated intimacy. And this is something that's going to happen across every ace or every phase of the funnel where there are these moments of heightened receptivity where your prospect would be that much more likely to ascend to the next. There's this moment where they are more receptive, more open, more ready. And this is where we'd be most wise to intervene with an invitation, a more personalized invitation, whether a message, whether a video message, whether a text message, something more direct and personal that would accelerate their transformation and invite them to evolve to the next ace or the next stage. Cool. So all of this, of course, without getting lost in the weeds is in service of the golden rule, which is that revenue is a direct function of influence and influence is a direct function of intimacy. So how do we create these more intimate exchanges at scale? Well, this whole program, this whole system is predicated on doubling 5xing or even 10xing the number of meaningful and transformational exchanges you're having with your pre-customers without burning yourself out, without literally cold messaging every lead in your ecosystem. So this is about identifying those who are most ready, receptive for that exchange. So to do that, we got to automate away the manual tasks in our funnel, but most importantly, and this is the core of the program, we need to find a way to signal who is closer to becoming a buyer and when they need that personal touch point to ascend to the next level and what exactly that touch point is. So all of these signals we're gathering is where the tech comes into play. This is where we're talking about things such as lead scoring based on some signals that your prospect is leaving us as they interact with your content, your funnel, the scaffolding of your marketing, the more static pieces, if you will. We're talking about behavioral triggers, we're talking about advanced segmentation, we're talking about sales pipelines, all of these things are going to be critical to giving us the opportunity to knowing who to intervene with in a personal direct way and when in one of those moments of heightened receptivity as we just spoke about before. So all of these tech pieces need to be built to give us that structure, to give us that ability to capture and act on that data and that is what Phil is going to be talking about throughout this program in his modules which are called the mothership. Cool, so the bottom line here is that successful coaching and course businesses are both becoming more automated and more human and intimate. The automation allows us to become more human and intimate, so it's not one or the other, it's actually both and the way I like to equate this is that prior to 2020 and maybe even longer before, there tended to be this idea of the guru on stage mentality and this is very common in the expert or the coaching or the course industry where the course creator or the coach would essentially have that guru on stage mentality. They'd be the one up here while everyone is down there, they're sucking energy from the crowd, they have this out of reach distant one-upsmanship attitude to them. If you look very closely or not even look very closely, you'll notice this narcissistic almost messiah complex which I guess you could say is inherent with something called the guru industry. But yeah, at the end of the day, they are exerting authority over their prospect which is a very outdated and commonly taught marketing concept from the 90s and early 2000s where you need to be the expert and you have the authority over your prospect and they need to feel helpless without you. So all these very disempowering elements that have been ingrained in a lot of marketers who grew up through that phase and of course all of this is very much ego driven. Well, where we are heading post 2021 is this idea of leading from within. So not a guru on the stage but someone really deep in the trenches with their prospect leading from within that group, shining your light, serving fiercely, being on that same level and just elevating everyone in your ecosystem through your presence, through your care and it's no longer about authority over your prospect but authority over your topic and no longer about being ego driven but being service driven. So these are likely the virtues that attracted you to the empire engineering community. These are things we talk about all the time and these are things that are increasingly more valuable, respected and rewarded from the coaching and the course industry. All right, so cool number one we're going to talk about today is touch point theory. So the idea here is that the more touch points and channels and mediums your prospect experiences you through, the more familiar they'll be with you and in turn the more they'll equate that with trust. So the simple way of saying this is intimacy ascension. So as you ascend in the intimacy of your channels, you get closer to offer ascension, the ability to present an offer, present an invitation and have it well received with a higher likelihood of being accepted. So the main reason cold traffic or a cold prospect doesn't convert isn't necessarily because it's someone new to you. It's because they've only experienced you through a single static video or a static page or a static medium. And what all this means is you're not yet real to them. And if you're not real to them, you can't have resonance, which is that X factory spoke about earlier. So the goal is to accelerate trust, familiarity and ultimately resonance by having your prospect experience you through mediums and channels that ascend what we call the paid scale, because we like acronyms that equate to terms that we like to use. So this means personal, alive, intimate and dynamic. And this is what it looks like. So on the bottom left, you'll see static, dead and impersonal, one too many. So the vertical line there is the intimacy, you have personal one on one, or you have one too many. And on the horizontal plane, you have static, dead. So these are assets that you essentially create, you know, one time and maybe run it for weeks, months or years, or they could even be things you created on the fly, like a video message more personally, but it's still static and dead, there's not a dynamic exchange. And on the right side, of course, you have dynamic and alive. That is something where there's actually an interaction and a conversation with your prospect, whether that's one to many, or one on one. So the goal here is that cold traffic will usually be getting something on the bottom left, these impersonal one to many static dead assets, like ads, landing pages, automated webinars, broadcast emails, videos, podcasts. And this is usually reserved for cold. And this is where you're earning potential lives. But if you want to move closer to that hot that warming them up and capturing that earned revenue, we're going to want to gradually move up to that top right where we increase in the dynamism of our exchanges, the aliveness of our exchanges, as well as the personal and intimacy aspects of it. So this is just a good broad overview of the different kinds of assets you'll be using. And ideally, as you get closer to the sale, moving in the direction of both more dynamic and alive and more personal and intimate. Cool. But the thing to remember here is that they all work together. It's not about saying things in the bottom left are wrong or things in the top left are wrong. All of them actually do have their place. But the goal is there is an upward aim from bottom left to top right. And I'll give you an example, a one on one direct message without having first experienced you through an ad or a Facebook post or a blog actually isn't that valuable. It may be high on the paid scale, but it's not preceded by an earlier exchange and therefore can feel intrusive and unwelcomed instead of valued appreciated and something accelerating that coaching the conversion element. So it simply comes down to channel switching, you know, and giving them an opportunity to channel switch into more and more intimate intimate rungs, more dynamic rungs of that scale. So an example of how this might play out is an ad point someone to an article. So those are static, they opt into your list, and they get a welcome email. So we're still pretty static, then we send them a personal loom video, which is more alive, but not dynamic. Then they get a lead magnet, which is more static, then there's a webinar often more static, and then we send them a direct message, a personal message or a personal text asking them what their biggest question is. So that is a little bit more personal webinar. Then we send them a text message after it. And then we maybe we get the modern one on one call to get to the close. So this is how we can mix all these elements on the paid scale, all these different channel switches, which build familiar familiarity, trust and resonance over time, and ultimately are all tools at our disposal for coaching that conversion. Cool. So we don't lower those, we don't discount those lower quadrants, the more impersonal ones. They show you're a leader to hundreds or thousands, which actually boosts both the real and the very perceived value of the more intimate channels. And the idea here is to leave your prospect with that sense of, oh, wow, they're literally talking to hundreds or thousands of people, they're a leader to this many people, and they're taking 90 seconds out of their day to send me a personal message, like that's the effect it has. And essentially, it's the difference between a subway busker taking five minutes to chat with you or a rock star coming out of the arena garage after performing for 20,000. So both those people are investing, they're time to chat with you, but there's a much higher perceived value for the one who has just performed for 20,000 and is in higher demand. Cool. So once again, it's important to leave you with the idea that they only ascend into the paid rungs of that scale through an equal exchange of energy and investment, which is the premise of earned enrollment. So let's talk more about this. So the conversion experience needs to feel balanced between their investment in us, and our investment in them. So for them to fully participate in the next ace or the next phase of the funnel, they need to feel as if they've earned it through a degree of what we call exclusive empowerment, something they have the opportunity to experience because of an action they took, while everyone else is not getting that experience because they haven't yet earned it. So we devalue marketing that's pushed on us, and we value and engage with transformation and value that is actually gifted to us through our actions. And the idea of this is the difference between push versus permission versus what I call partner marketing. So practically speaking, your pre-customer earns a more intimate, valuable, and invested exchange through their own actions. And this has many applications in your funnel. So you approve their entry into a Facebook group based on their answers. You qualify them on that. You offer them a direct conversation or personal reach out when they earn it with a certain engagement, via maybe downloading or consuming a lead magnet or watching a video. You grant them access to an enrollment call or a demo call or a sales call if their application qualifies them. And you extend an invitation to work with you or join your program based on what they share on that call. So everything across every stage of the funnel from beginning till end to customer is all invitation based. And it's all based on extending that invitation if they qualify, if they've earned it through a certain exchange. And this is where we become partners instead of pushing marketing on them. We actually partner with them through our marketing one step at a time. Cool. So at every juncture, every channel switch, every A switch, we're reflecting back the previous investment they've made and how and why they've earned entry into the next container. So it's not enough for us to just know that they've earned their spot in the next phase of that funnel or the next ace. We actually want to reflect that back. We want to reflect back that commitment they just made. We want to reflect back the value they just invested in our relationship. We want to reflect back the virtues that we see in them or the aspects or characteristics we see in them that actually was the reasoning between us partnering back with them to make that investment. Cool. So last tool, last major tool at least is identifying conversion context. So these are moments I call moments of heightened receptivity where your prospect has just done or participated or experienced something that qualified them for the next rung. So maybe they just requested a lead man. If they just signed up for a webinar, they just commented on a post or purchased something or attended a webinar. So all these things have a timed aspect to them or a timely aspect to them. And the best moment to invite someone to ascend is literally when they've just invested themselves mentally, emotionally or energetically in the transformation via some form of commitment. So for a clean definition, moments of heightened intent and receptivity where a personalized and dynamic exchange can exponentially accelerate the speed of conversion from top to bottom or middle to bottom of the funnel. So in a hyper distracted world, the key to significant conversion rate optimization is actually to intervene with intimacy during these crucial context or ascension moments. And I'm going to give you the example of a marriage proposal. So you could have the exact same couple with the exact same goals, desires, history together, and readiness to move forward in their lives. And there could be two different contexts for that marriage proposal. One of them could be while at the in-laws for Thanksgiving dinner, two hours after a big triggering blow-up where the in-laws were just grilling you on all these aspects of your career that make you feel disempowered and just grilled and like you were in an FBI interrogation. So there could be a marriage proposal an hour after that, or there could be a marriage proposal, you know, at a Bali sunset when you're on vacation and you just had an empowering day working at a co-working space and then going for an epic hike going on a waterfall adventure or whatever it is, right? And you have this moment of intimacy and stillness and quietness. And then you pop the question. So same couple, same goals, same ambition, same love for each other, very different contexts. And that's what we're looking for in these moments of heightened receptivity is to intervene during the Bali sunset, not at the weekend at the in-laws. Cool. So there are three types of conversion context for you to look for. One is top of mind when they know who you are. So this is the moment they just joined your group, the moment they just subscribed to your list when this is still so fresh in their mind. They haven't looked at other competitors yet. They haven't had that action diluted by a million other things. You are exactly what they have just been thinking about in that moment. And there was some motivation and desire to join your world. So the next is signaling intent. So these are precursors to a purchase or the next desired action. So this is where they maybe just signed up to your list through an intent-based lead magnet landing page where they often signals a pain or problem they want to have fixed or a pleasure they're seeking. So they requested or download or access to lead magnet. Once again, shows high intent or requested or consumed a specific video or even better signed up for a webinar. So these are all things they just did that signaled intent as a precursor to a potential purchase. And then finally, signaling readiness to be coached towards completion. So this is where someone is asking or attending a webinar and asking questions on it. That's a high intent action. Booking a sales call, of course, is extremely high intent. Visiting a sales page more than three times, opening several sales emails and engaging with an offer post or a handrails. So these, once again, are conversion contexts that would align with bottom a funnel race number three. Cool. All right. Ooh, slide drama. All right. So in short, we're looking for the subtle tells and conversion signals that tell us if someone is going to become a customer. And ideally, even before they know it, which is what I like to call minority report marketing. When our marketing becomes so intelligent that it could pick up on these tells that align with someone who is empowered and close to being a customer almost before even they do. So all these tools will be baked into our conversion playbooks, which are coming up next. But first, the real next is the mothership with Phil Powis, where he'll be leading you into actually building out some of these systems that can notice this data within your CRM record it. So you have this ever growing log of it and your business is becoming more intelligent literally by the day with every action that every prospect is taking. And you get to act on it with these playbooks that we're going to be getting into later. So I'll leave you with that. Grab a sip.
